name: Yash Rastogi
li_headline: Takes agentic AI from prototype to production · Built Amadeus’s first enterprise AI platform · Azure OpenAI · LangChain · Python
li_about:
I didn't plan to become an AI Engineer.

I joined Amadeus as a frontend developer - Angular, travel portals, airline integrations. I led a team, shipped features, and got good at the craft. But somewhere in between, I started asking a different question: what happens when you replace the manual parts with intelligence?

That question led me to Flutter, then to agentic AI, and eventually to building something no one at Amadeus had built before.

In 2024, I took on the task of designing the company's first production agentic AI platform from scratch. The problem was real: enterprise travel agencies were spending 30+ minutes manually processing each travel request - reading emails, searching flights, building proposals. We automated the entire workflow. The system reads unstructured travel requests, reasons over them using multi-step LLM orchestration, and returns personalised flight and hotel proposals - delivered asynchronously through email and API.

What made it interesting wasn't just building it. It was making it work at enterprise scale:
- Cut LLM inference costs by ~60% by rethinking how context is structured and passed to the model
- Reduced response time by 50% by parallelizing agent execution and redesigning the planning layer
- The architecture was adopted by 5 internal teams as the reference implementation for agentic AI at Amadeus

The hardest problems weren't the ones I expected. Latency,  inference cost, and output reliability - the three things that  kill AI systems in production - each required deep analysis with  no obvious answer at the start. That's the work I find most  interesting, and the work I'm best at.

I work across the full stack of production AI: Python, Azure OpenAI, Semantic Kernel, LangChain, LangGraph, RAG pipelines, prompt engineering, Azure Functions, Event Hubs, Cosmos DB, Docker, and CI/CD. I care about systems that actually ship - not models that stay in notebooks.

Outside work, I build independently. AI-powered mobile apps, live on Google Play with paying subscribers. A profitable algorithmic trading bot monitoring 200 Indian stocks in real time. I find it hard to stop building things.

If your team has built an AI prototype and needs someone who  has been through the pain of making it production ready -  reliable, cost-efficient, and scalable under real load - I'd like to talk.

name: Pramodh  N
li_headline: RF & Wireless Systems Test Engineer - RADWIN
li_about:
I am a Radio Frequency Engineer with over three years of professional experience in RF integration, wireless communication systems, and network performance validation across defence and commercial wireless domains. My career has been focused on delivering reliable, high-performance communication systems through structured testing, deep RF analysis, and systematic troubleshooting.

Currently at RADWIN, I work on system-level validation and performance benchmarking of advanced wireless communication products operating on Linux and VxWorks platforms. My responsibilities include designing and executing comprehensive RF and antenna test plans for PTP and PTMP solutions, analyzing critical performance metrics such as RSSI, CINR, MCS, throughput, and latency, and performing detailed root cause analysis to ensure maximum link stability and uptime. I actively monitor deployed networks using cloud-based NMS platforms, perform link budget analysis using planning tools, and leverage Wireshark, SNMP, Jperf, Linux CLI, and Python for protocol-level debugging and traffic evaluation. My role requires strong coordination across cross-functional teams to resolve complex technical issues and enhance product reliability.

Prior to this, at Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), I worked extensively with RF measurement and integration systems supporting defence communication platforms. I gained hands-on expertise with high-end RF test equipment including the R&S CMA180 Radio Test Set, Keysight PXA Signal Analyzer, spectrum analyzers, oscilloscopes, power meters, and RF attenuators. My contributions included testing and configuration of communication systems for defence vehicles, satellite communication projects involving VSAT and BUC systems, and manual PCB validation. I was also involved in ensuring compliance, signal integrity, and system-level functionality across mission-critical applications.

In addition, I possess foundational knowledge of LTE 4G and 5G RAN technologies aligned with 3GPP standards, including understanding of MAC, RLC, RRC, and PDCP protocol layers, EPC architecture, and end-to-end network behavior. My technical strengths include RF troubleshooting, link optimization, spectrum analysis, network configuration, performance monitoring, and systematic fault isolation.

I am passionate about advancing wireless technologies and building resilient communication systems. I continuously seek opportunities to enhance my technical depth in RF systems, next-generation wireless networks, and high-reliability communication infrastructures.

name: Karan Saini
li_headline: Growth Strategist | Brand & GTM for early-stage SaaS & IT | Helping teams scale with strategy + storytelling
li_about:
I was 21, a fresh face in a big world, stepping into my first full-time role. No fancy title, no big talk—just a head full of dreams and a heart that refused to settle.

Fast-forward 10 months—I'm proud (and a little overwhelmed) to say I was named Elcom Digital's Most Valuable Person and Elcom Superstar of the Year. 

What changed?

Honestly, I didn’t. I just stayed hungry, stayed real, and showed up every day with the same fire I started with.

At 22 now, I find myself working alongside top leaders and management teams across India, helping shape growth strategies and build momentum in the IT world. 

It's surreal some days—to sit in rooms I once dreamed of, having conversations that move the needle.

But more than the roles or the recognition, what really drives me is people. Understanding them. Learning from them. Building with them.

I’ve always believed that entrepreneurship isn’t just about founding companies. It’s about finding meaning. It’s about seeing problems not as obstacles, but as invitations. 

And it’s about connecting dots between what people need and what the world is waiting for.

🌱 From a curious new joiner to a valued teammate.
🧠 From asking questions to driving conversations.
🚀 From dreaming about impact to slowly, steadily creating it.

I’m still just getting started—but I carry every lesson, every failure, and every high-five with me. 

Because behind the growth charts and strategy decks, there’s just a young guy who loves to build, loves to learn, and loves to listen.

If you’re on a mission, or just love a good story—let’s talk. Because every great idea starts with a conversation.

Backend and ML engineer with 4+ years building data-intensive systems — from event-driven ingestion pipelines on AWS to neural-network recommendation models that moved real product metrics.
Currently at Guidewire, I architect and lead IP3 — the Iceberg-tier sink in our 3-stage CDC pipeline (DB WAL → Kafka/Debezium → Iceberg) — serving 400+ DP-IDs across 100+ tenants and 700+ tables. Recent shipping highlight: replaced 40 per-DP-ID Lambdas with a unified Spark job using a single Iceberg commit, eliminating commit contention and cutting data-gap detection runtime ~20x. Also reduced an Iceberg cleanup job from a 48+ hour timeout to under 40 minutes by resolving race conditions with concurrent writers.
Previously at CaaStle, I worked across the AI/ML stack — built a three-tower neural network recommendation model (10% lift in closeting rate), embedding-based product recommendations using LangChain and GPT-4V (5% engagement lift), and AI-generated fashion content via LoRA fine-tuning on Stable Diffusion. Also led the Collection Generation pipeline, cutting runtime from 5 hours to 30 minutes.
Before that, I built secure React Native flows for PIN reset and money add/withdrawal at Groww, used by 70M+ users.
What I care about: diagnosing the architectural root cause (commit contention, race conditions, throttling logic) rather than throwing more compute at the symptom. I enjoy taking ambiguous infra problems from POC through to team-wide adoption.
Stack I work in: Python · Java · Apache Spark · Apache Iceberg · Kafka · AWS (Lambda, EventBridge, Glue, Athena, EMR Serverless, SageMaker) · Elasticsearch / ELK · LangChain · PyTorch · Spring Boot.
Open to senior backend, ML, and data-platform roles. Bangalore or remote.

Full forwarding unit, flush logic, hazard resolution across EX and MEM stages — 19 modules, ~765 lines of RTL, simulated at 10 MHz. Before that, a single-cycle RV32I core supporting 12 base integer instructions across R, I, S, and B types — ALU, register file, control logic, datapath, all from scratch. Before that, a Space Invaders clone with a custom VGA sprite engine on Altera DE2 — 640×480 output, debounced controls, game-state FSM — entirely in RTL, no software layer in between.
 
On the verification side, I write testbenches, trace signals, and find the bugs that compile cleanly and simulate quietly — until they don't. Currently working through UVM methodology with Synopsys VCS and Verdi as my primary debug environment.
 
EDA Stack: Synopsys — VCS · Verdi · Fusion Compiler · SpyGlass · Custom Compiler · PrimeWave 
Cadence — Virtuoso 
Open Source — Icarus Verilog · Verilator · GTKWave · Yosys · OpenLane · Qflow · OpenROAD
 
Credentials: Synopsys/Samsung ISWDP Cohort 7 Fellow Smart India Hackathon — National Finalist IIT PALS InnoWAH 2025–26 — Runner Up CGPA 8.78 — Rajalakshmi Institute of Technology
 
I've also worked across embedded systems — LiDAR driver firmware in Embedded C on TI hardware with interrupt-driven UART at 115200 baud and a custom ring buffer parser, IoT sensor pipelines on Raspberry Pi 5 and ESP32, edge compute with Azure integration. Different domain, same instinct: understand the hardware first, then build on top of it.
 
The bug that only shows up 10,000 clock cycles in? That's the interesting one.
 
Currently in my pre-final year, graduating 2027. Open to internships and full-time roles in RTL Design, Functional Verification, and FPGA Engineering — India and beyond.
 
GitHub: github.com/Navnofficial

Because before people enquire, buy, follow, refer, or remember you, they first need to understand you.

What do you offer?
Who is it for?
Why should they trust you?
What makes your brand different?
Why should they choose you?

name: Pranith Jain
li_headline: Investigate. Automate. Eliminate. | · Open-Source (OSINT) Investigations · Security Automation · Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI)  | Certified AI Security Expert & Cyber Criminologist
li_about:
Every incident I've worked has the same shape: something got through, and the team had to figure out how.
 
After 250+ phishing, BEC, and malware investigations, I stopped just closing the incident. I started closing the gap that let it happen. That's the loop I run on everything: investigate it deeply enough to understand the attacker's logic, then build the automation that removes the manual step from the next one.
 
My core focus is Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) and threat analysis. I work across the full intelligence cycle: collecting and processing threat data from OSINT sources and RSS threat feeds, analyzing adversary Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs), mapping findings to MITRE ATT&CK, and delivering actionable intelligence that shifts teams from reactive remediation to proactive threat hunting. OPSEC is built into how I work, not an afterthought.
 
At Qubit Capital I defend email infrastructure across 150+ startups, 1,300+ domains, 2,700+ inboxes, real-time. I pushed DMARC alignment to 98%+ across the portfolio, cut incident response from 4 hours to under 75 minutes with n8n pipelines, and built a live infrastructure dashboard using Claude Code that replaced manual health checks entirely. I've stopped 30+ lookalike domain campaigns before they hit an inbox using proactive domain abuse monitoring and OSINT-driven attacker tracing.
 
Outside the day job I'm building a full-stack DFIR platform covering IOC enrichment, indicator correlation, phishing analysis, exposure scanning, and threat intel feeds. I'm a member of M3AAWG and the UK OSINT Community, and I write on email security and threat defense for DevX.
 
Currently deepening my skills in Security Automation, API Security, LLM Security, and Non-Human Identity (NHI). I actively monitor threat actor infrastructure, track emerging attack patterns, and follow intelligence feeds to stay ahead of the threat landscape. Specifically focused on how LLM-based attacks are breaking traditional detection models and what the modern defensive stack needs to look like.
 
I believe in active, community-driven learning. I'm a Trial Ambassador at APIsec University, engaging with the API security community and staying current on evolving attack surfaces. I participate in open threat intelligence communities, OSINT groups, and hands-on security challenges to keep skills sharp and thinking fresh.
 
Open to roles in Cyber Threat Intelligence, Threat Analysis, OSINT Investigation, DFIR, or Security Automation.

name: Soikat Chakrabarty
li_headline: Senior Software Engineer | React, React Native, Next.js | Performance, Scalability & Developer Experience
li_about:
I didn’t start out chasing titles or buzzwords — I started out chasing understanding.

Over the past ~7 years, I’ve been building high-performance, production-ready software for fully remote, async-first teams, learning the hard way how systems break, how products scale, and how small technical decisions quietly shape user experience.

Along the way, I found my craft in the frontend — React, React Native, Next.js, and TypeScript — not because it’s flashy, but because it sits at the intersection of performance, design, and real human behavior. This is where messy requirements, global users, and imperfect networks all collide.

Today, I work remotely with globally distributed teams across India, the UAE, and Europe. At PropertyFinder (UAE), I help build and maintain a large-scale B2B platform used daily by real estate agents. I own features end-to-end across web and mobile — from design discussions to production releases — tackling the kinds of problems that don’t show up in demos: bloated bundles, brittle deployments, flaky tests, and incidents that demand calm thinking under strict SLAs.

Some of the dragons 🐉 I’ve had to slay:
• Shrinking frontend bundles by 65%+ to restore performance and trust
• Shipping reliably through CI/CD instead of heroics and late nights
• Turning fragile flows into stable ones with Playwright automation
• Debugging production issues across timezones, without panic or blame

I’ve learned that the best teams don’t move fast by talking more — they move fast by writing clearly, trusting each other, and giving engineers real ownership. That’s why I thrive in async environments that value focus over meetings, documentation over assumptions, and long-term product thinking over short-term hacks.

Outside day-to-day product work, I enjoy exploring frontend system design, React internals, and performance-driven UI architecture — mostly because the next dragon is always hiding somewhere in the stack.

If you’re building a product-led company with a strong remote culture and room for deep, thoughtful engineering, I’d be glad to cross paths.

name: Ajinkya S. Mahajan
li_headline:
Whole Life Carbon Specialist
                                                                
I measure the carbon locked in before your building opens
                                   
Turner & Townsend l India & APAC
li_about:
Most commercial buildings in India and APAC have never seen their lifetime carbon figure.  
Not a rating. Not a benchmark comparison. The actual number from material extraction through construction, operation, and demolition that determines whether a building is genuinely low-carbon or just certified that way.  
That number is usually between 100,000 and 300,000 tonnes of CO2e for a commercial tower. And the majority of it is determined by structural decisions made in the first three months of design. No operational measure touches it after the fact.  
I build that number and the reduction pathways before the window closes.  

What I deliver: 
→ Whole Life Carbon Assessment (WLCA, A-D) :- RICS-aligned, GHG Protocol-grounded 
→ Embodied carbon reduction strategies :- built from real supplier EPDs, not database averages 
→ Cost-carbon trade-off models that convert carbon data into investment decisions 
→ Net zero roadmaps for real estate portfolios with verified baselines  

Outcomes on live projects: 
▸ 50% embodied carbon reduction on a 70,000+ sqm commercial tower 
▸ 35% reduction potential identified on a 256,000 tCO2e lifecycle baseline occupied building 
▸ 10,000+ tonnes CO2e reduced across 20 sites for a global energy company 
▸ 90% construction waste diversion verified, documented, ESG-reportable  

Currently at Turner & Townsend, leading net zero and sustainability initiatives projects across India and APAC. IGBC AP | RICS WLCA practitioner | One Click LCA  

Open to senior WLCA consulting roles, embedded sustainability engagements, and carbon advisory for commercial developers across India, APAC, UK, and Australia.

Building HOOD — The most obvious idea nobody has properly executed in India yet.
When did MRP start meaning Maximum — not Manufacturer's?
That ₹200 on the label? The manufacturer got ₹100. The rest went to middlemen who added nothing but margin.
HOOD cuts them out. Apartment communities connect directly to vendors through group buys led by Captains — verified residents who organise orders and earn for doing it. Neighbours save 20–40% on things they buy every month anyway.

I also invented HeliCop HC-1 — an autonomous drone that flies ahead of ambulances and clears traffic in real-time. Every second an ambulance is stuck is a second someone doesn't have. (Patent filed, March 2026.)

Founder of Woof — a social and rewards platform (in development) that turns everyday dog walks into real-world adventures. I’m building it to get people — and their dogs — outside, moving, and meeting IRL, making every walk more connected, fun, and rewarding. I’m obsessed with products that bridge tech, community, and real-world action; Woof is the first step — a movement for owners who want more than just another app.

Whether it was walking into Accenture's Bengaluru office as a software engineering intern, working remotely with a Scottish startup to build partnerships across three continents, or freelancing as a content creator on the side - I've never fit neatly into one box. And honestly, I've stopped trying to.
 
Right now, I'm at Coforge as a Graduate Engineer Trainee, working in a consulting capacity where I help bridge the gap between what technology can do and what businesses actually need. It's the kind of role where no two days look the same - and I love that.
 
Beyond my current role, I'm actively exploring the finance world, because I believe the most interesting problems of the next decade will live at the intersection of technology, data, and money.
 
Oh, and here's something most people don't put on LinkedIn - I'm a storyteller. Always have been. I believe the best ideas in the world die because nobody could explain them well enough. Give me a complex problem, a whiteboard, or even just a coffee chat, and I'll find a way to make it click for anyone in the room.
 
When I'm not working, I'm learning. Data Science, Machine Learning, financial systems - I'm constantly adding tools to my toolkit.
 
If you want to talk tech, strategy, finance, or just hear a good story - let's connect. I promise it'll be worth your time.
